_comment: Project-local MCP server pinning for ConciergeTravel.fr.
Servers listed here are the ones every contributor (human or agent) should
have available. User-level config still applies on top — these entries
merely document the project's expected toolkit.
We DO NOT embed secrets here. Each server reads its credentials from the
contributor's user-level Cursor config (`~/.cursor/mcp.json`) or from
environment variables exported in the IDE. Reference docs:

_documentation.rationale: These five MCP servers are the project's preferred operational surfaces: Supabase (read-only DB introspection + migrations), Cloudinary (media), Resend (transactional email previews), shadcn (component catalogue), Tavily (web research). For Sentry, Vercel, GitHub, Datadog, Opsera, Sanity, and Superhuman, rely on the user-level config (`~/.cursor/mcp.json`).
_documentation.secrets: Never commit secrets here. Every `${VAR}` is interpolated from the contributor's shell environment. Example: export SUPABASE_ACCESS_TOKEN before launching Cursor.
_documentation.scope: Project-level MCP files are a thin contract. The full toolkit (Sentry, Vercel, GitHub, Datadog, ...) stays at the user level so we don't fragment credentials across machines.
